meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Scale Your Student Projects with AI
content.seo_description: Overcome scaling limitations by mastering SaaS starter kits. A professional, advanced-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop reinventing the wheel. A SaaS starter kit provides a pre-architected foundation—authentication, database schemas, and payment integration—allowing you to focus on unique value rather than boilerplate. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you can bypass entry-level friction and ship production-ready, scalable software in days.
content.intro:
You have the vision, but your code is trapped in a cycle of repetitive setup. Every time you start a new idea, you lose three days just configuring Auth0, Tailwind, and Stripe. The 'blank page' syndrome isn't just about design; it's about the technical debt you accrue before you've even written a single line of business logic.

True engineering isn't about writing every line from scratch; it's about orchestration. By adopting a SaaS starter kit, you treat your codebase like a product, not a homework assignment. It is time to stop building projects that die in local environments and start shipping architectures that scale, using the same professional tooling used by senior engineers.

steps.title: Define Infrastructure Schema
steps.description: Establish the backbone of your SaaS. We define the database relationships and API requirements before writing code to ensure long-term scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a scalable SaaS database schema for a multi-tenant application using PostgreSQL and Prisma. Include user roles, subscription tiers, and project-specific data isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize Prisma schema based on the provided Antigravity architecture.'

steps.title: Configure Authentication Layer
steps.description: Implement robust, secure authentication. This step ensures your user management is production-ready from the first login.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth.js with Supabase adapter for a multi-tenant environment. Include middleware for route protection and session management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement NextAuth session handling and protect routes in the middleware.ts file.'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Gateway
steps.description: Hook into Stripe to handle subscriptions. This is critical for moving from a 'project' to a 'business'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler for subscription events (created, updated, canceled). Map events to the user database model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create the Stripe webhook route handler in the API directory and verify the signing secret.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Decouple deployment from release. This allows you to test new features in production without impacting your user base.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a feature flagging service that integrates with environment variables and allows toggling features per user ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a feature-flag utility module and integrate it into the UI components.'

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Set up an automated testing and deployment pipeline so your code is always in a deployable state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for a Next.js project that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate the .github/workflows/main.yml file with automated test and build steps.'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema in a separate 'schema-definition' file to keep your ORM clean.
Use 'Environment Variables' for everything; never hardcode credentials, even in your local dev environment.
Implement 'Soft Deletes' for all user-generated content to prevent catastrophic data loss during early testing.
Treat your logs as first-class citizens: integrate an observability tool like Axiom or Sentry on day one.
Focus on 'Vertical Slicing': complete the full user flow (Auth -> DB -> Stripe -> Deploy) before adding secondary features.
content.next_steps: After completing these steps, deploy your starter kit to Vercel or Railway. Then, begin adding your unique value proposition as a modular feature inside the new architecture.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same architectural patterns used by professional SaaS engineers. Stop the cycle of starting over and start building for longevity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
